risk_if_not_implemented: Without Competency Requirements for Security-Related Positions, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Assigned Security, Compliance & Resilience Responsibilities
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to assign one or more qualified individuals with the mission and resources to centrally-manage, coordinate, develop, implement and maintain an enterprise-wide Security, Compliance & Resilience Program (SCRP).
compensating_control_1.justification: Assigned Security, Compliance & Resilience Responsibilities (GOV-04) provides resilience and recovery capability that compensates for the absence of Competency Requirements for Security-Related Positions (HRS-03.2) by ensuring the organization can restore operations and data when the primary control is absent. Given the people-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Defined Roles & Responsibilities
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to define cybersecurity roles & responsibilities for all personnel.
compensating_control_2.justification: Defined Roles & Responsibilities (HRS-03) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Competency Requirements for Security-Related Positions (HRS-03.2) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with People applicability. Given the people-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
